senses.definition: the act of organizing something before a main event or process begins
examples: We started preorganizing the files to save time later.
Preorganizing the workspace helped the team work more efficiently.

senses.definition: dating or describing something before the original version; existing prior to an original form
examples: The preoriginal design inspired later versions.
Researchers studied the preoriginal sketches to understand the concept.

senses.definition: before the original form or version; in a way that precedes the original
examples: The concept was preoriginally developed in the 19th century.
Preoriginally, the notes were sketches rather than full plans.

senses.definition: before or preceding ornamentation or decorative features
examples: The ceiling had preornamental planning before any paint was applied.
They kept the walls plain with preornamental guidelines.

senses.definition: relating to the forward movement or position of the eye (rare, specialized usage)
examples: The preotic adjustment affected how the display was viewed.
In ophthalmology, preotic measurements can inform alignment.

senses.definition: an outfit or clothing prepared in advance for a specific event
examples: She bought a preoutfit for the gala.
The team received a preoutfit kit to wear during the event.

senses.definition: already equipped or clothed with an outfit before use
examples: The team arrived preoutfitted for the ceremony.
The kit was preoutfitted with all required items.

senses.definition: the act of equipping or dressing in advance
examples: Preoutfitting saves time on event day.
They discussed preoutfitting during planning.

senses.definition: a rough, early sketch or plan outlining main points before a fuller outline
examples: They created a preoutline to map the talk.
The lecturer prepared a preoutline before writing the full outline.

senses.definition: drawn or drafted in advance as an outline
examples: The preoutlined sections guided the final document.
A preoutlined layout helps with structure.

senses.definition: the act of creating an outline in advance
examples: Preoutlining the speech helped keep it focused.
During preoutlining, they decided on three main points.

senses.definition: to have overthrown someone or something earlier in time
examples: The rebels preoverthrew the old regime in a prior uprising.
They preoverthrew the plan before the final vote.

senses.definition: an act or instance of overthrow occurring before another event
examples: The preoverthrow of the regime set the stage for negotiations.
Historians discuss possible preoverthrow attempts.

senses.definition: the act of overthrowing something in advance or beforehand
examples: They were preoverthrowing the current power dynamic by early reforms.
The strategy involved preoverthrowing resistance.

senses.definition: overthrown at an earlier time; toppled before another event
examples: The preoverthrown government was replaced quickly.
Preoverthrown leaders still influenced the talks.

senses.definition: the stage before oviposition (egg-laying) in biological terms
examples: In the insect study, measurements were taken during preoviposition.
Preoviposition behavior was observed before egg-laying.

senses.definition: before ovulation in a biological cycle
examples: Preovulatory hormones rise just before ovulation.
The chart tracked preovulatory changes.

senses.definition: owned before by someone else; previously owned goods
examples: They bought a preowned car with a warranty.
The shop sells preowned electronics at a discount.

senses.definition: short for prepared or preparation; also a class of educational program (prep school) or a prefix in words
examples: He studied with a SAT prep course.
The prep for the event took all morning.

senses.definition: to prepare something in advance
examples: We need to prep the materials before the meeting.
They prepped the stage for the performance.

senses.definition: to pack something in advance or before a main process
examples: They will prepack the gift for shipping.
The team prepack the materials for the demo.

senses.definition: to package something beforehand, often for distribution
examples: The company will prepackage samples for the conference.
Prepackage kits reduce wait times at checkout.

senses.definition: already packaged in advance for sale or distribution
examples: The prepackaged meals are ready to heat and eat.
Prepackaged software bundles save time.

senses.definition: instances of packaging something in advance
examples: The warehouse sends prepackages daily.
Prepackages are prepared to speed delivery.

senses.definition: the process of packaging something in advance
examples: Prepackaging reduces delays at the pickup point.
We reviewed the prepackaging steps for efficiency.

senses.definition: packed in advance; already packed
examples: The prepacked lunch was convenient for the trip.
Prepacked items reduce handling time.
